free_text: first version of data.
Second version of data.
Elastic and inelastic scattering of fast neutrons
from iron, nickel and tungsten
5.5 MV pulsed beam Van de Graaff
accelerator with Mobley-type beam buncher.
T(p,n)He-3.
The energy spread of the neutrons was 90keV
Time-of-flight.
Areas of unresolved peaks in the energy spectra were
determined by the peeling-off method.
2-nd ed.
.Data analysed using optical-model, Hauser-Feshbach
and Moldauer theories. Best-fit sets of optical-model
parameters given in NP/A,125,641,1969.
Data from priv. comm., K.Tsukada. Some of the
data received May 1967 (1-st version) have been
replaced by the 2-nd version received March 1968.
.Corrections for multiple scattering, neutron-flux
attenuation, source-to-sample geometrical effects in
the scattering samples and polyethylene by the Monte-
Carlo program MULT1.
Errors indicated contain statistical errors
and errors originating in the peeling-off procedure
when it is applied.
Additionally should be taken into account:
The uncertainty of the efficiency is
mainly caused by the uncertainty in the 3H(p,n) cross
section, which is estimated to be 10 % above 2 MeV and
may amount to 20 % below 1 MeV.
A.P.T. Japan code changed to JPN
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections in all Subents. EN-RSL moved to COMMON in
Subent 001. ANALYSIS -> ADD-RES. EN-RSL -> EN-RSL-HW.
INC-SPECT was added.
